node.title: Do we actually need math for LeetCode, or is it just flex? 🤔

article.title: Do we actually need math for LeetCode, or is it just flex? 🤔
article.content:
Body:
I’ve noticed a split on LeetCode discussions:

~40% say math is the core skill (number theory, combinatorics, probability).
~50% argue patterns + DS (DP, graphs, sliding window) solve 90% of problems.
~10% think it depends on the company or role.

So what’s the truth? Are math-heavy solutions really necessary, or just a flex to look “smart”?

👉 Drop an example problem where math was unavoidable — or prove it’s mostly patterns.
👍 Upvote if you want more people to weigh in and settle this once and for all.

post.content: Basic elementary math is required like modulo properties, PnC(very basic), not of JEE level at least for LC for cracking companies.

I\'m representative of these 10% - it depends on the company. For example to be hired at Google you basically need to solve 3 of these medium-hard leetcode problems and you\'re good. From what category are these questions - it\'s pure luck, so it doesn\'t really matter if math-based problems appear only 20% of time. If you got one and you don\'t know that property you need to solve it - you\'re screwed. So I guess you need to know it all xD. On the other hand, there are companies for whom that leetcode round is just a screening with easy problems and more focus is directed towards checking if you know design patterns, technology, programming languages etc.
